summary: Add 'drafts get <id>' and 'drafts send <id>' subcommands. The send subcommand fetches the current server-side draftValue via /v3/userdata.read and dispatches it via the existing Gmail-API path, so any edits a user makes in the Superhuman UI between draft creation and send are honored.
reason: 2026-05-22 Esper-to-Gradle send: an agent created a draft via 'drafts new', the user edited it in the Superhuman web UI, and the agent then called 'send' with the original body it had on file. 'send' built a brand-new draft from local --to/--subject/--body and dispatched THAT, silently discarding the UI edits. The CLI had no drafts get / drafts send verbs, no path to ship an existing draft by id. New subcommand mirrors the drafts discard shape (positional draft-id arg, hidden namespace, resolveActiveAccount/readDraft helpers, JSON envelope). Skips step1WriteMessage on purpose: re-writing risks racing with another active client (Superhuman web) that may have just persisted a fresh edit.
validated_outcome: drafts_send_test.go TestDraftsSend_HonorsServerSideBody asserts the Gmail wire payload carries the server-side draft body bytes, not anything the caller composed. Round-trip: drafts new -> edit body in Superhuman web -> drafts send <id> ships the edited body.
